channel.title: The Grant Desk - EU, next 90 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: EU, next 90 days.

item.title: Medialab Matadero: Collaborative Prototyping Lab - LAB 5 'The Mechanics of Truth' (Madrid)
item.description:
Organisation: Medialab Matadero

Location: Madrid, Spain (in-person, intensive two-week residency)

Award: Travel, accommodation and meals covered for participants residing outside the Community of Madrid; access to Medialab Matadero's prototyping facilities; public presentation of developed projects at the OpenLAB festival. Free to participate. No application fee.

Duration: Two-week residency (12-25 November 2026) followed by public presentation at the OpenLAB festival (26-28 November 2026)

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Fifth cycle of Medialab Matadero's Collaborative Prototyping Lab, dedicated to exploring how truth is constructed, mediated and contested in contemporary society. The programme supports a wide spectrum of approaches, from highly technical to deeply speculative, and cultivates critical, collaborative prototyping within Madrid's contemporary landscape. Projects developed during the residency are presented publicly as part of the OpenLAB festival immediately afterwards. ELIGIBILITY: practitioners worldwide, aged 18 and older, across visual cultures, technology, architecture, journalism, activism, science and beyond; both project leads and collaborators welcome to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AEIP: Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Africa-Europe Innovation Partnership (AEIP), implemented by The African Youth Café (TYC), funded by the European Union

Location: Hybrid (online training plus field engagement; applicants based in Africa, Europe or the diaspora)

Award: EUR 3,000 stipend per fellow to cover content production costs (transport, data, basic fieldwork needs), plus structured digital-storytelling training, editorial mentorship and platform access for publishing work. No application fee.

Duration: Six months (June to December 2026)

Deadline: 29 May 2026

Hybrid digital storytelling fellowship from the Africa-Europe Innovation Partnership (AEIP), implemented by The African Youth Café and funded by the European Union, supporting young creatives to document and share stories of youth innovation across Africa, Europe and the diaspora through writing, photography, video, audio and digital storytelling. ELIGIBILITY: applicants aged 18-35 with an interest in storytelling, media or digital content creation, based in Africa, Europe or the diaspora, and able to commit to the full programme. IMPORTANT: as of May 2026 the African applicant quota has been reached - only European applicants are currently being accepted. FORMAT: online training sessions combined with field engagement for content production, plus continuous editorial mentorship and platform access for publishing. The canonical AEIP page does not list a fixed stipend amount, so confirm on application; the Terra Viva summary cites EUR 3,000. Apply via the Microsoft Forms application linked from the AEIP call page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ars Electronica: State of the ART(ist) 2026 (Linz)
item.description:
Organisation: Ars Electronica, in cooperation with the Austrian Federal Ministry for European and International Affairs

Location: Linz, Austria (international open call; project presented at the Ars Electronica Festival)

Award: Main Prize EUR 6,000 plus Award of Distinction EUR 4,000. Both selected projects are presented at the Ars Electronica Festival (9 to 13 September 2026, Linz). No application fee.

Duration: One-off prize plus festival presentation

Deadline: 24 May 2026

International open call by Ars Electronica for artists whose livelihoods and working conditions are severely affected by political repression, war, restrictions on freedom of expression, environmental crises, or economic and systemic exploitation. ELIGIBILITY: individual artists and collectives working in artistic, cultural, social, academic, activist or interdisciplinary fields; proof of risk or instability is a baseline eligibility requirement and projects are then evaluated on artistic quality.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Council of Europe: New Democratic Pact for Europe Democracy Hackathon 2026 (Strasbourg)
item.description:
Organisation: Council of Europe (under the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers), in partnership with No Hate Speech Week and Microsoft

Location: Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, France (hybrid: four online warm-up sessions leading to a three-day in-person finale)

Award: EUR 50,000 Microsoft-funded prize pool distributed among winning teams. Travel and accommodation covered for the 20 selected finalist teams. No application fee.

Duration: Online warm-up sessions plus three-day in-person hackathon (17-19 June 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

Council of Europe civic-tech hackathon to combat online hate speech and develop tech solutions for democracy, run alongside No Hate Speech Week and backed by a Microsoft prize pool. Four challenge tracks cover privacy-preserving hate speech detection, identifying coordinated inauthentic behaviour, gender-based violence reporting systems, and innovative social transformation approaches. ELIGIBILITY: pre-formed multidisciplinary teams of 3-4 (typically a technologist/developer, a democracy or policy expert, and a designer or UX innovator); residents of the 46 Council of Europe member states; coding experience is not required for every team member. FORMAT: four online warm-up sessions, then a three-day in-person finale at the Palais de l'Europe, Strasbourg, 17-19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Sonic Acts: The Walls Have Ears - Sound Studies Writing Residency 2026 (Amsterdam)
item.description:
Organisation: Sonic Acts (with De Ateliers / Woonhuis)

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands

Award: EUR 3,000 artist fee plus accommodation in Amsterdam, up to EUR 500 EU travel reimbursement, and Sonic Acts Biennial tickets. No application fee.

Duration: One-month writing residency in Amsterdam

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Funded one-month sound studies writing residency in Amsterdam from Sonic Acts at De Ateliers / Woonhuis. This is a residency for sound-art practitioners and scholars who write about sound (sound studies, criticism, theory) - it is a writing residency, not a sound-production residency. ELIGIBILITY: EU-based artists, researchers and scholars with at least one prior publication in sound studies or sound-art writing.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mozilla Festival (MozFest) 2026: Call for Proposals - Wilding
item.description:
Organisation: Mozilla Foundation

Location: Barcelona, Spain (Recinte Fabra i Coats; co-hosted with the City of Barcelona as part of Open Tech Week)

Award: Limited travel support, not guaranteed: flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform; a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla; a 5-day Metro pass including airport transfer. No additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance. Visa fee reimbursements handled through a separate process. NO artist fee, stipend or honorarium.

Duration: Three days, 28-30 October 2026

Deadline: 31 May 2026

MozFest 2026 is Mozilla Foundation's annual festival of debates, labs and workshops, forums, showcases, talks and installations - this year at Recinte Fabra i Coats in Barcelona (28-30 October 2026), co-hosted with the City of Barcelona during Open Tech Week. 2026 THEME 'WILDING': a provocation about loosening rigid systems and creating conditions for communities and technologies to evolve collectively, naturally and intuitively. Inspired by ecological rewilding, it explores community-owned mesh networks, federated platforms, open protocols, community archives, and tools built on transparency, trust and mutual flourishing. Mozilla is inviting proposals that exemplify Wilding and celebrate community-led alternatives - work that is proof of what is possible. FESTIVAL TRACKS: Wilding Advocacy; Wilding Knowledge; Wilding Systems; Wilding With Nature; Youth Wilding; Wilding Creativity; Founders Wilding; Developers Wilding. TRAVEL SUPPORT (limited; the festival cannot guarantee support for every applicant): flights to Barcelona booked through Mozilla's platform, a local hotel room reserved by Mozilla, and a 5-day Metro pass including the airport. Mozilla cannot provide additional funds for food, other ground transport or travel insurance; visa fee reimbursements are handled through a separate process. There is no artist fee, stipend or honorarium. PROCESS: submit a proposal via the MozFest CFP on the Mozilla Foundation festival site; travel support requests are reviewed after the CFP closes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Passage Art-Residency-Programme 2026 (KOBRA Cultural Network)
item.description:
Organisation: Polygona Kunstverein e.V. (KOBRA Cultural Network)

Location: Wittenberg region, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany

Award: Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice.

Duration: Four weeks, October 2026

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Two residency places in the Passage Art-Residency-Programme run by Polygona Kunstverein e.V. as part of the KOBRA Cultural Network, which explores continuities and discontinuities in the cultural landscape between Flaeming and Duebener Heide in the district of Wittenberg (Saxony-Anhalt, Germany). National and international artists working in photography and film are invited to apply. The two 2026 residencies run in October 2026 in collaboration with KOBRA partners Ferropolis Foundation for Industrial Heritage and the Research Library of Reformation Studies Wittenberg (RFB). TOPIC ONE (Ferropolis): artistic perspectives on the history of the former Zschornewitz coal-fired power station, the transformation of industrial landscapes, and visions for the future of post-industrial areas. TOPIC TWO (RFB): exploring the research library as a store of knowledge and examining the production of knowledge and its visual representation, drawing on a collection of around 230,000 volumes including Reformation-era first editions, manuscripts and historical writings. ELIGIBILITY: minimum age 18, no age limit; students are not eligible. Artists are selected by jury on the quality of submitted work; selected artists notified from 20 June 2026. FUNDING per place: accommodation for the four-week stay, a EUR 5,000 grant, and an additional EUR 1,000 for childcare if attending with a child. OBLIGATIONS: four weeks present in October 2026, realisation of an artistic project, provision of ten project photographs by 31 December 2026, delivery of a one-day workshop for young people, a public artist talk, and two days participating in filming for a documentary. Artists retain copyright and ownership of works created. Childcare and child-friendly accommodation can be arranged; partners may also accompany the artist at the artist's own additional cost. No application fee. Apply via the online form at passageair.org by 30 May 2026; applications accepted in German or English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Explora - Academie des beaux-arts European Award 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Art Explora and Academie des beaux-arts

Location: Europe (EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the Western Balkans, and Ukraine)

Award: Three award categories based on the organisation's 2025 annual expenditure: Category 1 (up to EUR 200,000 expenditure) - EUR 30,000; Category 2 (EUR 200,000 to EUR 1 million) - EUR 40,000; Category 3 (over EUR 1 million) - EUR 50,000. All applicants automatically become eligible for the Special Jury Award and the Audience Choice Award, each worth EUR 30,000.

Deadline: 26 Jun 2026

Annual award from Art Explora and the Academie des beaux-arts, open to all non-profit European cultural organisations. The European Award encourages new forms of audience engagement and participation in arts and culture, supporting innovative projects across all art forms that can be shared, replicated and scaled across Europe. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be non-profit cultural institutions or organisations based in an EU member state, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland, the United Kingdom, the Western Balkans (Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Republic of North Macedonia, Kosovo, Montenegro, Serbia), or Ukraine (eligible as of 2025). Applicants apply to one of three categories based on their 2025 annual expenditure, with awards of EUR 30,000, EUR 40,000 or EUR 50,000 respectively; all applications are automatically considered for the Special Jury Award and the Audience Choice Award (EUR 30,000 each). Timeline: call open 16 March to 26 June 2026; shortlist committee 4 September 2026; final jury 14 October 2026; award ceremony 24 November 2026. To discuss a project, applicants may write to prix@artexplora.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Creapolis: Malta 2026 - Diversification through Culture
item.description:
Organisation: Creapolis (B Creative Tracks)

Location: Malta (open to Ukrainian professionals currently based in Ukraine)

Award: Fully funded: all travel, accommodation and programme costs are covered.

Duration: One week, 5-9 October 2026

Deadline: 04 Jun 2026

One-week immersive programme in Malta on 'diversification through culture', examining how cultural value is produced, negotiated and redistributed across institutions, public space, tourism, education and community networks. The edition focuses on processes, decision-making structures and systemic dynamics in a dense, interconnected cultural ecosystem, where culture operates simultaneously as heritage, contemporary practice, community expression, public service and economic activity. Programme themes: diversification of cultural practice across heritage, craft, contemporary arts and community traditions; hybrid governance models; culture as a driver of tourism and place-based economies; cultural transmission and identity-building; collaboration between public authorities, civil society and practitioners; adaptive cultural strategies in resource-constrained environments. Participants get hands-on experience, practical tools and collaborative methodologies, exchange with local experts and European peers, exchange with other Ukrainian professionals, and access to a European network for continued cooperation. ELIGIBILITY: Ukrainian professionals currently based in Ukraine, engaged in community and civic development through culture, education, innovation or entrepreneurship - including civil servants and local policymakers, educators, researchers and social innovators, cultural and creative entrepreneurs, and community organisers and local development actors. Applicants should demonstrate clear motivation, relevant experience and a commitment to applying insights to their local Ukrainian context. Working language English. All travel, accommodation and programme costs covered.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: MedSEA: Socially Engaged Arts in the Mediterranean - Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Larnaka 2030, Al Badil, Liv.in.g and Busart (Creative Europe consortium)

Location: Residencies in Larnaka (Cyprus) and Sfax (Tunisia); applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe-eligible country

Award: Full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and access to an international network of artists and cultural professionals.

Duration: Multi-phase programme October 2026 - May 2027: capacity-building phase plus a four-week residency (Larnaka 5 October - 1 November 2026; Sfax 5 April - 2 May 2027)

Deadline: 05 Jun 2026

MedSEA (Socially Engaged Arts in the Mediterranean Area) is a Creative Europe cooperation project where artistic practices engage directly with communities, territories and the social transformations shaping the Mediterranean. This open call selects 12 emerging artists and cultural professionals working - or wishing to work - with participatory and socially engaged artistic practices. It is a process of co-creation in real contexts rather than a traditional residency: participants work directly with local communities developing territory-rooted artistic projects. The programme runs in phases - a preparation and capacity-building phase (webinars and workshops on community-based artistic practices, sustainability and cultural project design), then a four-week residency of fieldwork and co-creation supported by project partners. Residencies take place in Larnaka, Cyprus (5 October - 1 November 2026) and Sfax, Tunisia (5 April - 2 May 2027); resulting works are presented at the Mahallart Festival in Larnaka and the Hors-Lits Festival in Sfax. ELIGIBILITY: artists and cultural professionals aged 18 or above, legally residing in a country eligible under the Creative Europe Programme, and actively engaged in community-based or socially engaged artistic practices across disciplines such as visual arts, performing arts, interdisciplinary practices and public space interventions. Selected participants receive full support: travel, accommodation, artistic fee, mentorship and network access. Implemented by a consortium of Larnaka 2030 (Cyprus), Al Badil (Tunisia), Liv.in.g (Italy) and Busart (Greece); co-funded by the Creative Europe Programme of the European Union. Deadline 5 June 2026, 23:59 CET. Contact: maria@larnaka2030.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: ifa: Rave Scholarship - Cultural Heritage and Museum Transformation (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen) - Alexander Rave Foundation

Location: Host institutions in Germany (applicants must be cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list)

Award: Net payment of EUR 1,500 per month; monthly family allowances for accompanying family (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance (including family); monthly public transport pass for the city of residence; up to EUR 500 for a language course.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Scholarship from ifa (Institut fuer Auslandsbeziehungen), supported by the Alexander Rave Foundation, on the management of cultural heritage and the transformation of museums and exhibition centres. It enables cultural practitioners from countries on the OECD DAC list to stay with host institutions in order to deepen their professional knowledge transnationally, introduce new perspectives and build long-term cooperation through exchange. The focus is on transcultural dialogue and engaging with cultural heritage, collections and archive records, funding mutual knowledge transfer and the creation of sustainable networks, and supporting freedom in art and science, freedom of opinion and open debates. Thematic focal areas centre on transforming museums and exhibition venues in line with the ICOM definition of museums (2023): cultural participation, sustainability, non-discriminatory spaces and decolonial working methods. ELIGIBILITY: curators, restorers, mediators and culture managers from DAC-list countries. BENEFITS: net payment of EUR 1,500/month; monthly family allowances (EUR 250 spouse, EUR 250 per child); visa costs; outward and return travel costs; health insurance including family; monthly public transport pass; up to EUR 500 for a language course. Application deadline 30 June 2026 for scholarships beginning after 1 February 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Magnetic 5: Franco-UK Artist Residencies (2026 call)
item.description:
Organisation: Fluxus Art Projects

Location: France and United Kingdom (artists from a French region apply to the associated UK residency; artists from a UK nation apply to the associated French region)

Award: Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities.

Duration: Two to three months (residency dates set per host)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Magnetic is a joint Franco-UK initiative coordinated by Fluxus Art Projects, bringing together ten institutions for a programme of 2-3 month artist residencies: five in France (Capc, Bordeaux; Frac Grand Large, Dunkirk; Frac Bretagne, Rennes; Villa Arson, Nice; Betonsalon, Paris) and five in the UK (Wysing Arts Centre and Gasworks in England; Flax Art Studios, Northern Ireland; Aberystwyth Arts Centre, Wales; Cove Park, Scotland). Artists from each of the five French regions apply for the associated UK residency; artists from each UK nation apply for the associated French region. Societal and environmental issues are at the heart of the programme; the residencies are situated, with selected artists expected to work in resonance with each host's geographical, historical, societal and artistic context.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Weaving Liberation: Digital Justice Fund (Pilot Round 2026)
item.description:
Organisation: Weaving Liberation (resourced by Ford Foundation, Luminate, and Oak Foundation)

Location: Europe (Council of Europe member states; at least 50% of the group must be based in one or multiple Council of Europe countries)

Award: Grants between €10,000 and €50,000 per group, to be used over up to two years (2026-2028). Total Fund budget for this first round: €500,000.

Duration: Up to two years of project work, between 2026 and 2028

Deadline: 24 May 2026

The Digital Justice Fund works towards nurturing an ecosystem where groups resisting violent technological harms and imagining more nourishing relationships with technologies have the resources they need to dream, organise, build and thrive towards liberatory digital presents and futures. We are interested in funding: Community building and sustaining around digital justice issues: community organising, peer-to-peer support, relationship-building activities, etc. Knowledge and capacity building: popular and political education, investigative journalism, skills exchanges, issue mappings (e.g., technological harms, community needs and desires), advancing liberatory narratives around technology, artistic interventions, and other creative approaches, etc. Coalition and movement building: advocacy, strategic litigation, campaigning, etc. Tech-related mutual aid: tech repair circles, peer support on digital safety and security, shared technical infrastructures for movement work, software/hardware sharing initiatives, etc. Life-affirming technology work: prototyping, piloting, or expanding community-centred, transfeminist, anti-colonial technologies, etc. Key dates: platform registration deadline Sunday 24 May 2026, 23:59 CET (registration is required by this date even if application is not yet complete); final application deadline Sunday 21 June 2026, 23:59 CET.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BrightBrain: Artist Residency 2026 (TU Braunschweig Science & Art Lab)
item.description:
Organisation: Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium

Location: Braunschweig, Germany (in-person residency at TU Braunschweig)

Award: Artist fee €2,400 per month (gross), plus travel costs and accommodation covered, plus production budget up to €3,500 (gross).

Duration: Autumn/winter 2026 residency (start from September 2026, exact start date flexible). Length agreed with host.

Deadline: 25 May 2026

Open call for an international artist-in-residence at the Science and Art Lab at TU Braunschweig, in cooperation with the BrightBrain research consortium, as part of the Ecoversity initiative. The residency aims to connect artistic and scientific perspectives. Artists are invited to propose their own research-based projects related to microphotonics, biomedicine, technology, neurodegenerative diseases, neurobiology, neuromorphic computing, and microelectronics (the current resident is collaborating with the Nitride Technology Center on light transmission in digital applications including micro-LEDs and neuromorphic computing). Strong fit for art-science, sensory translation, critical-data, and neuroscience-informed practices. Eligibility: international artists; should show interest in biomedicine, technology and innovation in their artistic work; be comfortable working in interdisciplinary environments; be motivated to collaborate with experts from science, culture and education; be willing to participate in two public events during the residency; and be able to communicate in English. Application by PDF to artist-residency@tu-braunschweig.de with subject 'BrightBrain residency' (if files exceed 25 MB, upload via the TU Braunschweig Nextcloud link in the call). Application deadline 25 May 2026. Jury decision expected end of June 2026; residency starts from September 2026 (exact date flexible). Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WIELS: Residency Programme 2027 (Brussels)
item.description:
Organisation: WIELS

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at WIELS contemporary art centre)

Award: Artistic recognition grant of €3,000 per selected artist (4 artists selected). Awarded as an artistic distinction without any production obligation or service in return. Under Belgian tax law, artistic prizes may be treated as miscellaneous income and may benefit from a partial tax exemption within certain limits (depends on the beneficiary's individual situation).

Duration: Six months in 2027, choose one block: January-June 2027 OR July-December 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Annual residency programme at WIELS (Brussels-based centre for contemporary art). This call is open EXCLUSIVELY to Belgian artists or artists who have been living and working in Belgium for at least three years, and who have contributed to the Brussels contemporary art scene during that period. An independent jury selects four artists, each awarded a €3,000 artistic recognition grant. The grant is a distinction supporting the quality of the artistic practice; there is no production requirement or contractual service obligation attached. Residency period is six months in 2027 (January-June or July-December block, applicant choice). Application deadline 31 May 2026. Application materials via online form: personal information; artistic approach (max 250 words); statement of intent describing projects, ideas and expectations for the residency (max 250 words); three professional references (name, email, phone, profession); and portfolio + CV via Option 1 (Dropbox/Google Drive link with up to 10 images/videos plus a numbered descriptive list with title, date, technique, dimensions and ~50-word description per item, plus CV) or Option 2 (PDF with up to 10 captioned images plus CV). Videos may also be hosted on YouTube/Vimeo (with password if needed); videos of still works are not accepted. All share links must be set to 'anyone with the link can view'; links requiring access requests will not be considered. Questions: residency@wiels.org (do not email about results, notifications will be sent). Free to apply. Note: this is a Belgium-restricted call and is not open to artists without a Belgian / 3+ years-in-Belgium connection.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ohme x EXPERIENCE: Open Call - Experience Lab (Digital Artist Residency on Consciousness)
item.description:
Organisation: Ohme (in collaboration with EXPERIENCE / CRCN, Université libre de Bruxelles)

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at Ohme's premises, USquare, Brussels)

Award: Artist fee: €5,300 (approx. 20 working days based on CP 304 salary scales, max 10 years experience). Production budget up to €2,000. Plus professional documentation, access to EXPERIENCE datasets/tools/documentation, preparatory reading list, Ohme team support, and workspace at USquare.

Duration: 20-day engagement spread between September and November 2026, with public restitution in December 2026. Preparatory period July-August 2026 (reading list and onboarding materials).

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Open call from Ohme (Brussels-based research and curatorial platform at the intersection of art and science) for a digital visual artist or collective to develop an artistic project in residence with EXPERIENCE, an ERC-funded research programme led by Prof. Axel Cleeremans at the Centre for Research in Cognition and Neurosciences (CRCN), Université libre de Bruxelles. EXPERIENCE investigates the nature of subjective experience (phenomenology) and builds 3D maps of 'phenomenal space' from large datasets of similarity and preference judgments. The selected artist works directly with these empirical datasets (similarity judgments, subjective preference data describing how people perceive, compare and value conscious experience) as primary creative material, not as illustration. Possible directions include information design that critically engages with visualising consciousness data; generative or algorithmic works using subjective valuation datasets as compositional grammar; data visualisation as artistic practice; creative coding exploring neural/latent space and perception/emotion/subjectivity; and critical or poetic approaches to measuring/mapping inner experience. Eligibility: visual artist or collective connected to the contemporary art field of the Fédération Wallonie-Bruxelles (live, work, or have a professional practice within the French-speaking art community of Belgium); established practice in digital art, new media, computational art, data visualisation or information design used as artistic practice; experience working with data as creative material (e.g. Processing, p5.js, openFrameworks, TouchDesigner, Max/MSP, or equivalent); critical engagement with the tools/systems behind information production and dissemination; interest in mind/perception/cognition/neuroscience; available in person in Brussels September-November 2026 and for the December restitution; English proficiency (working language); French and/or Dutch appreciated. Ohme is NOT looking for artists who already work on consciousness or neuroscience, but practitioners whose existing methods could be mobilised in this scientific context. Sample EXPERIENCE datasets: https://osf.io/suqkp/overview?view_only=fdc73f1485664a2c9c7571e0c62abe6a (paper: 'Exploring the role of micro-valence in the phenomenal space', https://doi.org/10.1093/nc/niag005). Selection by interdisciplinary jury of EXPERIENCE and Ohme representatives; interviews with top three candidates by phone/video/in-person in late June 2026; selected artist announced late June 2026. Application: single PDF in English to opencall@ohme.be (subject: 'Application Experience Lab') by 31 May 2026, 23:59 CET, including up-to-date CV (English), portfolio (English), 1-page motivation text (English) on interest in the residency and consciousness theme plus technical competences, and the completed contact form (downloadable from Ohme's site). Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CAIS Research Sabbaticals (Fellowships) 2027-28
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Advanced Internet Studies (CAIS), Bochum, Germany

Location: Bochum, Germany (residential at CAIS; rent-free, fully furnished apartment provided plus private office)

Award: Sabbatical leave on full salary: additional €600/month grant. No regular income: €2,000/month grant. Regular income below €1,400/month: top-up to the full grant. Alternative: reimbursement of salary or substitute costs within reasonable limits. €100/month extra per child under 18 for fellows on a full or compensatory grant. CAIS also covers one return trip to Bochum (or a daily commute if local), provides a rent-free fully furnished apartment and a private office. Fellows can request financial support for research expenses, invite a Visiting Fellow for up to 3 weeks of collaboration, and invite up to three European experts for half-day workshops (CAIS covers travel, accommodation and a daily allowance of up to €24).

Duration: 3 or 6 months. 6-month fellowships usually start in October or April; 3-month fellowships start in October, January, April or July. Exceptions possible by arrangement.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Residential research sabbaticals at CAIS, explicitly open to excellent scholars AND practitioners across all career stages and disciplines (not academia-only). Funds individual projects on the societal impact of digital transformation, including pure research and applied projects that develop new theories, methods or perspectives for practice. Project must be self-contained with specific milestones and produce an independent output suitable for short-timeframe publication: peer-reviewed paper or conference contribution, book chapter, policy paper, or prototype. Fellows join a vibrant interdisciplinary research community with regular joint activities (breakfast Tuesdays, colloquium and dinner Wednesdays, occasional workshops Thursdays) and an international network of alumni, working groups and affiliates. In Germany, full and compensatory grants are not subject to social security contributions and are usually tax-exempt; fellows resident abroad should verify their own tax position. Note: CAIS is currently reviewing the application format and selection process for the next call, so details may change. Next call publishes at the beginning of June 2026; deadline 23 July 2026 for fellowships in the period October 2027 to March 2028. Contact: Dr. Esther Laufer, esther.laufer@cais-research.de.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: falía* AIR 2026 Artist Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Associazione falía*

Location: Lozio, Valle Camonica, Northern Italy

Award: €70/week stipend (2 to 6 weeks) + shared accommodation and workspace + co-production support

Duration: 2 to 6 weeks (summer)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Summer artist residency in the mountain village of Lozio for individuals and collectives to create site-specific artworks, with preference for Land Art, Street Art and participatory art. Residents receive a weekly stipend, shared accommodation, workspace, cultural activities and co-production support; selected artworks become donations to the Municipality.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Festival SCAN: 4a Residència de Creació i Investigació at L'Arxiu Tarragona
item.description:
Organisation: Festival SCAN & Centre d'Imatges de Tarragona / L'Arxiu

Location: Tarragona, Spain

Award: €900 + 5 nights free accommodation + access to 1.5 million photographs and audiovisual materials

Duration: Per project (with public presentation in November 2026)

Deadline: 24 May 2026

Residency call for artists, researchers and curators to develop artistic or research projects around archival photographic and audiovisual media at the Centre d'Imatges de Tarragona. Selected applicants receive €900 in project support, free accommodation for up to 5 days, and access to a 1.5 million-strong archive. Resulting work must be publicly presented in November 2026 in a format of the artist's choosing.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Fediversity (11th call)
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Fediversity (funded by EU Horizon Europe)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for projects making federated and self-hosted internet services easier to deploy and operate at scale. Funds practical work on portable Fediverse-style infrastructure, hosted Mastodon-like or PeerTube-like services, secure email, private cloud and other tools that support decentralisation and digital self-determination. Open to individuals and organisations of any type; results released under free or open-source licences.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Journalismfund Europe: European Cross-Border Grants
item.description:
Organisation: Journalismfund Europe

Location: Europe (cross-border projects)

Award: Variable, typically €5,000 to €50,000 per project

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 21 May 2026

Supports independent investigative journalism across Europe, well-suited to surveillance, AI, platform governance and data-driven investigations. Cross-border team requirement: at least two professional journalists (freelance or staff) from at least two different countries. Next round after this one expected 2026-06-26.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist-in-Residence: Reimagining AI at the EU Policy Lab
item.description:
Organisation: Joint Research Centre, European Commission

Location: Brussels, Belgium (with possible site visit in Ispra, Italy)

Award: €15,000

Duration: 6 months

Deadline: 22 May 2026

Open call for an artist residency reimagining AI at the EU Policy Lab. The 15,000 EUR contracted budget covers expert fees, allowance, accommodation and travel during on-site work.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIT-PLU Residencies 2027 (4 host institutions across NL, IT, ES)
item.description:
Organisation: SIT-PLU consortium: Baltan Laboratories (NL), Lungomare (IT), Idensitat (ES), ZEMOS98 (ES), with LUCA School of Arts (BE), Floating University (DE), EINA (ES), Universitat Politècnica de València (ES). Funded by Creative Europe.

Location: Choose one host context: Wondermash / Meuse Delta (NL), Bolzano riverscapes (IT), Besos river sacrifice zones (ES), Galicia energy transition (ES)

Award: €13,500 artist fee (travel excluded) + production budget (varies by host)

Duration: 1 year residency starting January 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Situated Creative Practices for the Pluriverse (SIT-PLU) is a Creative Europe cooperation project tackling socio-ecological challenges through cross-disciplinary research and context-specific artistic interventions. Drawing on the Zapatista concept of the pluriverse ("a world where many worlds fit") and buen vivir, the project foregrounds communal interdependence and human/more-than-human relationships. Selected residents engage with one specific context for one year and develop new forms of creative intervention/cultural mediation. Four host contexts available for 2027: Baltan Laboratories with Wondermash (Meuse Delta, NL), Lungomare on Bolzano riverscapes (IT), Idensitat on sacrifice zones near the Besos river mouth (ES), ZEMOS98 on local sustainable energy-transition models in Galicia (ES). Two-phase application: Phase 1 deadline 31 May 2026 23:59 CET (motivation letter, CV, portfolio of up to 5 projects, draft proposal targeting one host) — results by mid-July; three shortlisted practitioners per host then develop a more specific proposal in dialogue with the host institution (Phase 2 deadline 14 August 2026, results end of September). Each host runs an online Q&A between 11 and 15 May 2026. Enquiries: sitplu.project@luca-arts.be.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI TALER (13th call): Privacy-Preserving Digital Payments
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI TALER (with GNU Taler community, funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (€676,000 total budget for open calls)

Duration: Project-based (programme runs to November 2026)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants for free and open source efforts aligned with privacy-preserving digital payments. Build new GNU Taler capabilities, auxiliary tools, UX, FOSS-app and open-standard integrations (P2P micropayments in messengers, social media, video conferencing), or merchant-backend improvements. All results released under a free or open-source licence. Strong projects can scale up via NGI0 Core.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NGI Zero Commons Fund (13th call)
item.description:
Organisation: NLnet / NGI Zero (funded by EU Horizon Europe and Swiss SERI)

Location: Worldwide

Award: €5,000 to €50,000 per project (scalable for proven potential)

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Small to medium-sized R&D grants to help reclaim the public nature of the internet across the full technology spectrum: libre silicon, middleware, P2P infrastructure, end-user applications, open standards, open data and AI, open science, creative commons and open educational resources. Project results released under free or open-source licences. Aimed at troubleshooters tackling hard, important internet-commons challenges. Total programme: €21.6m through 2027.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
